userStories.title: Create a pnpm TypeScript monorepo
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the project organized as a pnpm monorepo with TypeScript-first tooling so that server, runtime, web, mobile, desktop, and shared packages can evolve together.
acceptanceCriteria: The repository uses pnpm workspaces
All first-party apps and packages build with TypeScript
Shared linting, formatting, and tsconfig rules are centralized
A root command can install, build, lint, and typecheck the full monorepo
userStories.notes: Added pnpm-workspace.yaml with apps/* and packages/* workspaces, scaffolded TypeScript-first server/runtime/web/mobile/desktop/shared packages, centralized tooling under tooling/{tsconfig,eslint,prettier}, and root build/lint/typecheck scripts. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-001-monorepo.test.ts coverage and verified with pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Create shared core packages
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want shared packages for protocol, auth, sessions, ports, providers, and UI primitives so that all clients and services use the same domain model.
acceptanceCriteria: The monorepo includes shared packages for protocol, auth, sessions, ports, providers, and UI
Server, runtime, and clients consume shared types instead of duplicating models
Package boundaries are documented
Changes in shared types are validated by typecheck across all dependent apps
userStories.notes: Added focused workspace packages for protocol, auth, sessions, ports, providers, and UI primitives under packages/*, kept packages/shared as a compatibility barrel, and rewired server/runtime/web/mobile/desktop to consume shared domain types directly. Documented package boundaries in docs/shared-package-boundaries.md, added .agents/ralph/tests/us-002-shared-core-packages.test.ts coverage for package presence/app consumption/boundary docs/typecheck wiring, and verified with pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Run a Remote Dev Console Server control plane
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a central server that manages authentication, hosts, workspaces, sessions, approvals, notifications, and port exposure so that all clients connect through one control plane.
acceptanceCriteria: The server exposes APIs for hosts, workspaces, sessions, approvals, and forwarded ports
The server supports real-time event delivery to connected clients
The server persists core metadata required to recover state after restart
Unauthorized access to protected resources is rejected
userStories.notes: Implemented a typed control-plane server in apps/server/src/index.ts with protected HTTP APIs for hosts, workspaces, sessions, approvals, forwarded ports, and notifications, plus an SSE event stream at /v1/events. Added JSON-backed persistence and recovery via configurable storagePath, expanded auth scopes for write and approval/notification access control, and added .agents/ralph/tests/us-003-server-control-plane.test.ts to verify unauthorized rejection, API coverage, real-time event delivery, and restart recovery. Verified with pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Install the runtime on a remote Linux host
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to install a runtime on a remote Linux machine so that coding agents can run next to the repository and services they need.
acceptanceCriteria: A documented install flow is available for supported Linux systems
The runtime can enroll with the server using a bootstrap token
The runtime reports version, health, and connectivity status
The install flow is safe to rerun on an already configured host
userStories.notes: Added a documented Linux runtime install guide in docs/runtime-linux-install.md and linked it from README.md. Extended the control plane with bootstrap-token runtime enrollment and status endpoints that persist host runtime version, health, and connectivity in apps/server/src/index.ts, and replaced the runtime scaffold in apps/runtime/src/index.ts with idempotent Linux install helpers plus enrollment/status reporting functions. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-004-runtime-linux-install.test.ts coverage for supported Linux docs, rerunnable installs, bootstrap enrollment, status reporting, and restart persistence, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Support local mode with the same runtime contract
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the same runtime model to work locally so that local and remote development follow the same session and workspace lifecycle.
acceptanceCriteria: A local runtime can register with the server or attach directly in development mode
Local workspaces support the same session APIs as remote workspaces
Providers do not require separate local-only implementations
Clients clearly distinguish local and remote hosts
userStories.notes: Extended apps/runtime/src/index.ts with local-mode helpers so a local runtime can either attach directly in development mode through the existing RuntimeSessionManager/provider adapter contract or register with the control plane through the existing host/workspace APIs. Updated apps/server/src/index.ts to persist host connectionMode and workspace hostConnectionMode plus local runtime metadata on normal host registrations, and updated the served/browser/mobile client models so local and remote hosts are labeled distinctly. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-005-local-mode.test.ts coverage for direct local attachment, local control-plane registration, same session APIs on registered local workspaces, shared provider adapter usage, and client-visible local/remote host distinction, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Register repositories as managed workspaces
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to register local or remote repository paths as workspaces so that sessions always execute against a known codebase.
acceptanceCriteria: A workspace can be created from a repository path on a registered host
Workspace metadata includes host, path, default branch, and runtime association
Invalid or inaccessible paths are rejected with a clear error
Users can list, inspect, and remove registered workspaces
userStories.notes: Extended apps/server/src/index.ts so workspace registration validates accessible git repository paths, derives a default branch from the repository when needed, and stores host/path/defaultBranch/runtimeAssociation metadata while preserving the existing workspace payload fields for compatibility. Added GET /v1/workspaces/:id and DELETE /v1/workspaces/:id alongside list/create behavior, updated .agents/ralph/tests/us-003-server-control-plane.test.ts to use a real repository path under the stricter validation, and added .agents/ralph/tests/us-006-register-workspaces.test.ts coverage for create/list/inspect/remove flows plus clear invalid-path rejection. Verified with pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Start and monitor coding agent sessions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to start sessions against a workspace and monitor them from web, desktop, or mobile so that I do not need to SSH into the machine to supervise work.
acceptanceCriteria: A session can be started against a chosen workspace and provider
Clients receive live status, logs, and output events
Sessions can be paused, canceled, and resumed
Disconnected clients can reconnect and recover active session state
userStories.notes: Extended apps/server/src/index.ts and packages/sessions/src/index.ts so sessions validate provider/workspace selection, start in a running state, persist ordered status/log/output events, expose session detail and session event history endpoints, and support pause, resume, and cancel lifecycle actions while streaming live SSE updates and reconnect snapshots that include active session state. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-007-start-and-monitor-sessions.test.ts coverage for start/monitor flows, live status-log-output delivery, pause-resume-cancel actions, and reconnect recovery, then verified with pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Support Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode providers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a common provider adapter interface so that Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode can all run through the same session model.
acceptanceCriteria: The runtime exposes a provider adapter interface
Claude Code can be launched and monitored through the adapter interface
Codex can be launched and monitored through the adapter interface
OpenCode can be launched and monitored through the adapter interface
Provider-specific failures are surfaced without crashing the session manager
userStories.notes: Added a shared provider adapter contract and registry helper in packages/providers/src/index.ts, then exposed it from apps/runtime/src/index.ts with a RuntimeSessionManager plus built-in Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode adapters that launch provider commands, map stdout/stderr/status into shared session events, and contain provider-specific failures as failed session results instead of crashing the manager. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-008-provider-adapters.test.ts coverage for the exported adapter surface, successful launch/monitor flows for all three providers, and failure isolation, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Create isolated worktrees per task
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sessions to optionally run in dedicated git worktrees so that multiple agents can work in parallel without stepping on each other.
acceptanceCriteria: A session can create a dedicated worktree before starting
Worktree metadata is linked to the session
Dirty worktrees are rejected by default unless explicitly allowed
Users can choose direct workspace mode for simple workflows
userStories.notes: Extended packages/sessions/src/index.ts with session workspace/worktree metadata and updated apps/server/src/index.ts so session creation now supports workspaceMode=direct|worktree, persists the selected workspace path on the session, creates dedicated git worktrees for worktree sessions, and rejects dirty source repositories by default unless allowDirtyWorkspace=true is explicitly set. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-009-isolated-worktrees.test.ts coverage for direct workspace mode, dedicated worktree creation, dirty-workspace rejection, dirty override, and persisted worktree metadata, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Review diffs, changed files, and patch summaries
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to review agent output from the client so that I can validate changes without leaving Remote Dev Console.
acceptanceCriteria: Clients can list changed files for a session
Clients can view diffs or patch summaries
Large diffs degrade gracefully through truncation or pagination
The change view clearly identifies added, modified, renamed, and removed files
userStories.notes: Extended apps/server/src/index.ts with session change-review APIs at GET /v1/sessions/:id/changes and GET /v1/sessions/:id/changes/patch, backed by git status/diff inspection of the session workspace so clients can list changed files, inspect per-file diffs, and receive patch summaries with explicit added/modified/renamed/removed classification. Added pagination for changed-file and diff listings plus maxBytes-based diff truncation for large patches, introduced shared session change/diff types in packages/sessions/src/index.ts, and added .agents/ralph/tests/us-010-review-session-diffs.test.ts coverage for changed-file listing, rename/remove detection, patch summaries, pagination, and truncation. Verified with node --import tsx --test .agents/ralph/tests/us-010-review-session-diffs.test.ts, pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Approve or reject privileged actions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want risky actions to require explicit approval so that agents cannot perform sensitive operations without review.
acceptanceCriteria: Providers can raise approval-required actions through a common interface
Clients can approve or reject pending actions
Approval decisions are stored in an audit log
Rejected actions are surfaced cleanly to the running session
userStories.notes: Extended packages/providers/src/index.ts with a shared provider approval callback contract and rejection error, then updated apps/runtime/src/index.ts so provider adapters can request privileged-action approval, record approval outcomes, and fail sessions cleanly when a reviewer rejects an action. Updated apps/server/src/index.ts so approval requests and decisions append to a persisted audit log and rejected approvals emit session log events for the affected running session. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-011-approve-privileged-actions.test.ts coverage for provider-driven approval requests, client approve/reject flows, audit-log persistence, and clean rejection surfacing, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Forward remote ports for previews and tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want built-in remote port forwarding so that I can access preview apps, dashboards, and dev services from any client device.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can manually forward a TCP port from a registered host
Clients can view all active forwarded ports for a workspace or session
Forwarded ports can be opened, closed, and expired
HTTP services are exposed through a managed URL
At least private and shared visibility modes are supported
userStories.notes: Extended packages/ports/src/index.ts with forwarded-port protocol/status metadata plus active/expired helpers, and updated apps/server/src/index.ts so forwarded ports can be created against registered hosts with private/shared visibility, filtered by workspace or session, opened/closed/expired through PATCH lifecycle actions, automatically expired when their TTL elapses, and exposed with managed URLs for HTTP/HTTPS services. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-012-forward-remote-ports.test.ts coverage for manual TCP forwarding, workspace/session active-port listings, open-close-expire flows, managed HTTP URLs, visibility modes, and automatic expiration, then verified with pnpm test, pnpm build, pnpm lint, and pnpm typecheck.

userStories.title: Auto-detect common development ports
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the runtime to detect common listening ports so that previews are easy to find and expose when needed.
acceptanceCriteria: The runtime can detect listening ports associated with active sessions or workspaces
Detected ports appear in clients with suggested labels when possible
Users can promote a detected port into a managed forward
Detected ports are not exposed externally until a user forwards them
userStories.notes: Extended packages/ports/src/index.ts with a distinct detected-port model plus protocol/label suggestion helpers for common development services, then updated apps/runtime/src/index.ts with listening-port detection helpers that normalize runtime observations into session/workspace-associated detected ports. Updated apps/server/src/index.ts to persist detected ports, expose list/inspect/report/promote endpoints at /v1/detected-ports, include detected ports in control-plane snapshots, and only create externally managed URLs when a detected port is explicitly promoted into a forwarded port. Updated apps/web/src/index.ts and apps/mobile/src/index.ts so client dashboards fetch detected ports and can promote them into managed forwards. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-013-auto-detect-common-development-ports.test.ts coverage for runtime detection, suggested labels, client visibility, promotion into a managed forward, and the rule that detected ports are not externally exposed before forwarding, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Provide a web client
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a browser-based client so that I can manage sessions, approvals, ports, and previews from any machine.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can sign in and view hosts, workspaces, sessions, and forwarded ports
The web client can stream live session events
The web client can review diffs and respond to approvals
The web client can open forwarded HTTP previews
userStories.notes: Expanded apps/web/src/index.ts from a stub into a typed web control-plane client with token sign-in, concurrent dashboard loading for hosts/workspaces/sessions/approvals/ports, authenticated live event streaming, diff review helpers, approval decisions, preview URL resolution, and a browser document renderer. Added a served browser client at / and /app via apps/server/src/web-client.ts plus apps/server/src/index.ts so the control plane exposes a usable web UI for session supervision, approvals, diff inspection, and forwarded HTTP previews. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-014-provide-web-client.test.ts coverage for the served web page, sign-in dashboard reads, live session-event streaming, diff review, approval response, and HTTP preview opening, and verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Provide a mobile app with React Native and Expo
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a mobile app built with React Native and Expo so that I can monitor work, approve actions, and open previews from my phone.
acceptanceCriteria: The mobile app supports authentication and host or session browsing
The mobile app receives live session state updates
The mobile app can approve or reject pending actions
The mobile app can open forwarded HTTP previews in-app or via the system browser
userStories.notes: Expanded apps/mobile into an Expo-backed React Native app with app.json, an Expo entrypoint, and a mobile screen that signs in with a bearer token, browses hosts or sessions, streams live control-plane events, responds to approvals, and opens forwarded HTTP previews in-app or via the system browser. Replaced the stubbed mobile package surface in apps/mobile/src/index.ts with a typed mobile control-plane client, dashboard/event state helpers, browse-item builders, and preview-opening utilities that mirror the server APIs. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-015-provide-mobile-app.test.ts coverage for Expo package/config wiring, authenticated host/session browsing, live session state updates from SSE, approval decisions, and forwarded preview opening, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Provide a desktop app
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a desktop app so that I can use Remote Dev Console as a primary control surface for both remote and local work.
acceptanceCriteria: The desktop app can connect to the server and manage sessions
The desktop app can also work with local runtime flows
The desktop app reuses shared packages from the pnpm monorepo
Users can switch between local and remote workspaces in one interface

userStories.title: Persist session history and resume across devices
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sessions to be resumable from web, desktop, or mobile so that I can move between devices without losing context.
acceptanceCriteria: Session metadata includes provider, workspace, timestamps, and current state
Clients can reopen an existing session from another device
Recovered sessions include recent logs and messages
Temporary disconnects do not destroy active session state
userStories.notes: Extended packages/sessions/src/index.ts and apps/server/src/index.ts so session summaries now normalize updatedAt/lastActivityAt/endedAt metadata, the control plane exposes GET /v1/sessions/:id/recovery for resumable session recovery with recent event history, and session activity timestamps stay current as logs, outputs, status changes, and rejection logs are appended. Added recovery helpers to apps/web/src/index.ts, apps/mobile/src/index.ts, and apps/desktop/src/index.ts so web, mobile, and desktop clients can reopen an existing session from another device. Added .agents/ralph/tests/us-017-persist-session-history.test.ts coverage for cross-device recovery, recent log/output replay, persisted metadata after restart, and temporary disconnect recovery, then verified with pnpm verify:ralph.

userStories.title: Send notifications when user attention is required
userStories.description: As a developer, I want notifications for approvals, failures, and completion so that I can react quickly when I am away from my main machine.
acceptanceCriteria: Users can opt in to notifications on supported clients
The system sends notifications for approval required, failed session, and completed session events
Notifications can deep-link into the related session where supported
Users can mute or customize notification categories

userStories.title: Maintain an audit log for sensitive actions
userStories.description: As a maintainer or administrator, I want an audit trail for session and access activity so that sensitive remote actions can be reviewed later.
acceptanceCriteria: The audit log records session lifecycle events, approvals, provider choice, and port exposure changes
Each entry includes actor, target, timestamp, and outcome
Authorized users can inspect the audit log
Critical security-relevant events are not silently dropped

userStories.title: Document self-hosting and development workflows
userStories.description: As a developer, I want clear documentation so that I can self-host the project, connect runtimes, and contribute to the pnpm monorepo.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation covers server deployment, runtime install, and client setup
Documentation explains the pnpm monorepo layout and developer commands
Documentation includes provider setup for Claude Code, Codex, and OpenCode
Documentation explains port forwarding behavior and security expectations
